diagrams.title: Logical Address Structure in Segmentation
diagrams.description: Shows the two-part logical address format used in segmentation
elements: Segment Offset (d) - displacement within the segment
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how CPU generates addresses in segmentation systems

diagrams.title: Address Translation in Segmentation
diagrams.description: Step-by-step process of converting logical address to physical address
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the complete address translation mechanism with validation

diagrams.title: Segment Table Entry Structure
diagrams.description: Structure of a single entry in the segment table
diagrams.purpose: Understanding what information is stored and used for memory management

diagrams.title: External Fragmentation in Segmentation
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how external fragmentation occurs with variable-sized segments
elements: Memory layout showing allocated segments of different sizes
Free holes between segments that are too small for new segments
After deallocation: multiple small free blocks that cannot satisfy large request
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the problem of external fragmentation and need for compaction
